questions.explanation: The series is related in the following manner Hence option C is correct.

questions.question: Select the option that is related to the fifth number in the same way as the second number is related to the first number and the fourth number is related to the third number. 4 ∶ 48 ∷ 7 ∶ 294 ∷ 6 ∶ ?
questions.explanation: The series is related in the following manner Alternative: 42 × 3 = 48 72 × 6 = 294 62 × 5 = 180 Hence option B is correct.

questions.question: Which two signs/numbers. should be interchanged to make the given equation correct? 512×64÷5+30 – 50=20 > दिए गए समीकरण को सही बनाने के लिए किन दो चिह्नों/संख्याओं को परस्पर बदलना चाहिए? 512×64÷5+30 – 50=20
questions.explanation: from option .B Given eqn 512×64÷5+30-50=20 By option [B] 512÷64×5+30 – 20=50 ⇒ 8×5+10=50 ⇒ 50=50 ⇒ L.H.S. =R.H.S. Hence option B is correct. > विकल्प B से दिया गया समीकरण 512×64÷5+30-50=20 विकल्प [B] से 512÷64×5+30 – 20=50 ⇒ 8×5+10=50 ⇒ 50=50 ⇒ बायाँ पक्ष = दायाँ पक्ष अतः विकल्प B सही है।

questions.question: Select the option that represents the letters that, when placed from left to right in the blanks, will complete the letter series P_GIO _ D _ IORDG _ O _ D G I O > उस विकल्प का चयन करें जो उन अक्षरों को दर्शाता है, जो रिक्त स्थान में बाएं से दाएं रखने पर अक्षर श्रृंखला को पूरा करेंगे P_GIO _ D _ IORDG _ O _ D G I O
questions.explanation: On pairing the series The first letter in the five letter repetition series is in increasing order. P DG I O / Q D G I O / R D G I O / S D G I O Hence option C is correct > श्रृंखला को जोड़ने पर पांच अक्षर पुनरावृत्ति श्रृंखला में पहला अक्षर बढ़ते क्रम में है। P DG I O / Q D G I O / R D G I O / S D G I O इसलिए विकल्प C सही है

questions.question: Statements are given followed by conclusions. Assuming the statements to be true, even if they seem to be at variance with commonly known facts; decide which of the conclusion logically follows from the statements. Statements: All cups are stars. Some stars are jugs. No jug is glass. Some glasses are tables. Conclusions: I. Some tables are stars. II. Some glasses are cups. III. No table is stars.
questions.explanation: ‘OR’ Hence, either conclusion I or conclusion III follows. And conclusion II does not follow a/c to any of the Venn diagram.

questions.question: Select the option set in which the numbers are related in the same way as are the numbers of the following sets [Note:Operations should be performed on the whole numbers, without breaking down the numbers into its constituent digits. Eg-13-operations on 13 such as addition/ subtracting/ multiplying etc. to 13 can be performed. Breaking down 13 into 1 and 3 and then performing mathematical operations on 1 and 3 is not allowed.] [62, 5, 8] [87, 7, 11]
questions.explanation: Sets are related in the following manner [62, 5, 8] = [8+5]×5 – 3=62 [87, 7, 11] =[11+7]×5 – 3=87 In the same way; [112, 8, 15] =[15+8]×5 – 3=112 Hence, option C is correct.

questions.question: Select the option that represents the correct order of the given words as they would appear in an English dictionary. 1. Mistaken 2. Missing 3. Mistyped 4. Mistrust 5. Mission 6. Mistress
questions.explanation: The correct alphabetical order in dictionary will be; 2. Missing 5. Mission 1. Mistaken 6. Mistress 4. Mistrust 3. Mistyped Hence, option D is correct.

questions.question: Select the option that represents the letters that, when placed from left to right in the following blanks, will complete the letter-series G­_AREGL_R_G_C_EGL_ _E > उस विकल्प का चयन करें जो उन अक्षरों को दर्शाता है, जो निम्नलिखित रिक्त स्थानों में बाएं से दाएं रखने पर अक्षर-श्रृंखला को पूरा करेंगे G­_AREGL_R_G_C_EGL_ _E
questions.explanation: On pairing the series. The third letter in the repeated word series is in increasing order. G L A R E / G L B R E / G L C R E / G L DR E Hence option B is correct > श्रृंखला को जोड़ने पर। दोहराए गए शब्द श्रृंखला में तीसरा अक्षर बढ़ते क्रम में है। G L A R E / G L B R E / G L C R E / G L DR E इसलिए विकल्प B सही है

questions.question: In a certain code language, BOOK is written as ZMMQ and PEER is written as LWWJ. How will TEAR be written in same coding language? > एक निश्चित कोड भाषा में, BOOK को ZMMQ लिखा जाता है और PEER को LWWJ लिखा जाता है। उसी कोड भाषा में TEAR को कैसे लिखा जाएगा?
questions.explanation: Words are related in the following manner In the same way, Hence option A is correct > शब्द निम्नलिखित तरीके से संबंधित हैं उसी तरह से, अतः विकल्प A सही है

questions.question: Three of the following four letter-clusters are alike in a certain way and one is different. Pick the odd one out. > निम्नलिखित चार अक्षर-समूहों में से तीन एक निश्चित तरीके से समान हैं और एक भिन्न है। बेजोड़ को चुनें।
questions.explanation: The three words are alike in some way. Hence option D is correct > ये तीनों शब्द किसी न किसी तरह से एक जैसे हैं। अतः विकल्प D सही है

questions.question: Select the option that is related to the fifth letter cluster in the same way as the second letter cluster is related to the first letter cluster and the fourth lettercluster is related to the third letter cluster: GPK :MSI::RHT:VKT∷ VDW : ? > उस विकल्प का चयन कीजिए जो पांचवें अक्षर समूह से उसी प्रकार संबंधित है जिस प्रकार दूसरा अक्षर समूह पहले अक्षर समूह से संबंधित है और चौथा अक्षर समूह तीसरे अक्षर समूह से संबंधित है: GPK :MSI::RHT:VKT∷ VDW : ?
questions.explanation: GPK: MSI::RHT:VKT:: VDW: ? In the same way Hence option A is correct > GPK: MSI::RHT:VKT:: VDW: ? उसी तरह से अतः विकल्प A सही है

questions.question: In a certain code language, SPIT is coded as USKW and COPY is coded as ERRB. How will MOCK be coded in that language? > किसी निश्चित कोड भाषा में SPIT को USKW और COPY को ERRB लिखा जाता है। उस भाषा में MOCK को किस प्रकार लिखा जाएगा?
questions.explanation: The words are related in a following manner Similarly; Hence option C is correct. > ये शब्द निम्नलिखित तरीके से संबंधित हैं इसी प्रकार; अतः विकल्प C सही है।

questions.explanation: ?? = 108+47=155 Hence option A is correct.

questions.question: Three of the following four triads are alike in a certain way as they are formed by performing same mathematical operations among themselves and thus form a group. Which triad does not belong to that group? > निम्नलिखित चार त्रिक में से तीन एक निश्चित तरीके से एक जैसे हैं क्योंकि वे आपस में समान गणितीय संक्रियाएँ करके बने हैं और इस प्रकार एक समूह बनाते हैं। कौन सा त्रिक उस समूह से संबंधित नहीं है?
questions.explanation: Sets are related in a following manner Hence except option A, all are related in a same manner. > सेट निम्नलिखित तरीके से संबंधित हैं अतः विकल्प A को छोड़कर सभी एक ही प्रकार से संबंधित हैं।

questions.question: Select the option that is related to the fifth term in the same way as the second term is related to the first term and the fourth term is related to the third term. 132∶10∷42∶5∷272∶ ? > उस विकल्प का चयन कीजिए जो पांचवें पद से उसी प्रकार संबंधित है जिस प्रकार दूसरा पद पहले पद से संबंधित है और चौथा पद तीसरे पद से संबंधित है। 132∶10∷42∶5∷272∶ ?
questions.explanation: Numbers are related in a following manner Alternative, [10+1]11 × 12 = 132 [5+1]6 × 7 = 42 [15+1]16 × 17 = 272 Hence option C is correct. > संख्याएँ निम्नलिखित तरीके से संबंधित हैं विकल्प, [10+1]11 × 12 = 132 [5+1]6 × 7 = 42 [15+1]16 × 17 = 272 अतः विकल्प C सही है।

questions.question: In a certain language CURD is written as 342184 and BREAD is written as 2181024. How will BUTTER be written in that language?
questions.explanation: Given Here pattern follows, Vowels ×2 & Consonants ×1 So, BUTTER will be written as 24220201018. Hence option B is correct.

questions.question: Which character will be on the opposite face of &, when the given sheet is folded to make a cube? > जब दी गई शीट को घन बनाने के लिए मोड़ा जाता है, तो कौन सा अक्षर & के विपरीत फलक पर होगा?
questions.explanation: > is opposite to #, @ is opposite to % And, $ is opposite to &. > >, # के विपरीत है, @, % के विपरीत है और $, & के विपरीत है।

questions.question: Select the option that is related to the third word in the same way as the second word is related to the first word. [The words must be considered as meaningful English words and must not be related to each other based on the number of letters/ number of consonants/ Vowels in the word.] Diet: Dietician:: High blood sugar:?
questions.explanation: Dietician is the doctor who specializes in the treatment of Diet. In same way; Diabetologist is a doctor who specialize in the diagnosis, treatment and management of diabetes and its associated complications Hence, option B is correct.

questions.question: Select the correct combination of mathematical signs to sequentially replace the * sign and balance the given equation. 76 * 34 * 23 * 12 * 6 * 21
questions.explanation: From option B = 76 – 34 – 23+12÷6 = 76 – 57+2 = 19+2=21 Hence option B is correct. > विकल्प B से = 76 – 34 – 23+12÷6 = 76 – 57+2 = 19+2=21 अतः विकल्प B सही है।

questions.question: In a certain code language DICTATOR is written as DROTATCI and GLIMPSE is written as GESPMIL. How will CONDEMN be written in that language? > एक निश्चित कूट भाषा में DICTATOR को DROTATCI और GLIMPSE को GESPMIL लिखा जाता है। उस भाषा में CONDEMN को किस प्रकार लिखा जाएगा?
questions.explanation: Given Here keep the first letter in first position & remaining positions are filled from the right side. In the same way; Hence option A is correct. > दिया गया यहाँ पहले अक्षर को पहले स्थान पर रखें और शेष स्थान दाईं ओर से भरें। इसी प्रकार अतः विकल्प A सही है।

questions.question: Looking at the picturepainted on the wall, Priya pointed towards a man and said that he is her brother’s paternal grandfather's only child.How is the man related to Priya? > दीवार पर लगे चित्र को देखकर प्रिया ने एक आदमी की ओर इशारा करते हुए कहा कि वह उसके भाई के दादा की इकलौती संतान है। वह आदमी प्रिया से किस प्रकार संबंधित है?
questions.explanation: The pictorial graph of Blood related is as fallows So, the man is father of Priya. > रक्त संबंधी का सचित्र ग्राफ इस प्रकार है अतः वह व्यक्ति प्रिया का पिता है।

questions.question: Find the number of triangles in the following figure.
questions.explanation: Total number of triangles = 10

questions.question: Match the following: Para Athlete Sports A) Avani Lekhara 1) Archery B)Harvinder Singh 2) Shooting C) Kumar Nitesh 3) Athletics D) Sumit Antil 4) Badminton
questions.explanation: Avani is a para shooter and won medals in the 2020 Tokyo Paralympics. Harvinder is a para archer and won a bronze medal in archery at the 2020 Tokyo Paralympics. Kumar Nitesh is a para-badminton player. Sumit is a para-athlete in the javelin throw and won a gold medal at the 2020 Tokyo Paralympics.

questions.question: The policy of ‘Security cell’ is related with-?
questions.explanation: The policy of ‘Security Cell” is related to Warren Hastings and Wellesley. Warren Hastings fought with Mysore and Marathas to achieve status equivalent to other Indian states. During this time the company tried to form buffer state [also known as Ring Fence] with an aim to protect its state from Afghans and Marathas. Thus, they agreed to protect Awadh onthe condition that the Nawab of Oudh will bear the expenses. The main aim of Wellesley was to enforce Indian states to rely on his army power. The British policies towards Indian states were- 1. Company’s struggle for equality with Indian provinces. [1740-1765 AD] 2. ‘Security cell’ or the policy of siege. [1765-1813 AD] 3. Subordinate separation policy. [1813-1857 AD] 4. Subordinate union policy. [1858-1935 AD] 5. The policy of equal union. [1935-1947 AD]

questions.question: Who among the following famous reformers wrote the books Gyana Yoga, Karma Yoga, and Raja Yoga?
questions.explanation: The ‘Patron saint’ of India, Swami Vivekananda is respected throughout the world as a spiritual genius. One of the quotes of him, “Truth can be stated in a thousand diff erent ways, yet each one can be true.” Raja Yoga, Karma Yoga, Bhakti Yoga and Jnyana Yoga are works of Swami Vivekananda.

questions.question: Prabha Atre is mainly related to which Gharana?
questions.explanation: Prabha Atre is a famous Indian music singer of Kirana Gharana of India. He has been awarded the Sangeet Natak Akademi Award in 1991, Padma Shri in 1990, Padma Bhushan in 2002 and Padma Vibhushan in 2022.

questions.question: Who among the following did not contribute to building the famous ‘Qutub-Minar’?
questions.explanation: Qutbuddin Aybak, the founder of the Delhi Sultanate, started construction of the Qutb Minar, Iltutmish added three storeys to the tower. The topmost storey was damaged by lightning which was rebuilt by Firuz Shah Tughluq. Firuz Shah Tughluq built two floors. Ghiyasuddin Tughluq contributed nothing to its construction.

questions.question: The “In spite of being Buddhist, Ashoka did not leave belief in Hinduism” validation of this fact is:–
questions.explanation: The coronation of Ashoka was held in 269 B.C. He was named as ‘Devanampriya,’ ‘Devanampriyadarshi’ in his inscription which means beloved of Gods or handsome by looks. This indicates his belief in Hindu religion

questions.question: Which of the following forms of martial arts is from Kerala?
questions.explanation: Kalaripayattu is the oldest martial art technique the world belonging to the state of Kerala. The development of Kung-Fu is also considered through this art. Kalaripayattu means to be trained for the battlefield. Gatka is the traditional martial art of the Sikhs. The government of Punjab has recognized the Indian martial art 'Gatka' as a sport. Silambam is a martial art technique, it belongs to the state of Tamil Nadu. Paika Akhada martial arts technique is related to the state of Odisha. In ancient times, they used to give military training to the farmers.

questions.question: Balphakram National Park is often referred to as the "Land of Spirits." Why is it given this title?
options: It is believed to be the home of mythical creatures and spirits by the local Garo community.
It is known for its misty landscape, which resembles a spiritual realm
The park has a high concentration of rare, ghost-like animals.
The park is famous for its ancient burial grounds of tribal leaders
questions.explanation: Balphakram National Park, located in the South Garo Hills of Meghalaya, is often referred to as the "Land of Spirits" by the local Garo tribe. This title stems from the deep spiritual and cultural significance the park holds for the Garo people. According to their beliefs, the park is home to various mythical creatures and spirits, which are an integral part of their folklore and traditions. The park's misty landscapes and dense forests add to its mystical reputation, making it not only a natural wonder but also a place of spiritual importance.

questions.question: According to the Census of 2011, which of the following states has the highest density of population per km2?
questions.explanation: Population density of Jharkhand is 414/km2 It is the 15th largest state by area and the 14th largest by population.

questions.question: Umiam Lake, one of the largest lakes in Meghalaya, is located near which city?
questions.explanation: Umiam Lake, also known as Barapani, is a man-made reservoir located about 15 km north of Shillong, the capital city of Meghalaya. It was created by damming the Umiam River and serves as a major source of water supply for the region. The lake is surrounded by lush green hills and offers a stunning view, making it a popular destination for tourists who enjoy boating, water sports, and scenic views. Because of its proximity to Shillong, Umiam Lake is often visited by those traveling to the city or exploring the nearby areas. It is one of the largest and most beautiful lakes in the state.

questions.question: The Deosai National Park, located in northern Pakistan, is connected to Ladakh in India by which pass?
questions.explanation: The Burzil Pass is a high-altitude mountain pass that connects the Deosai National Park in northern Pakistan to the Ladakh region of India. It is one of the key routes between these regions, located in the Karakoram Range. The pass is significant because it is a trade and cultural link, and it offers a passage between the Indian subcontinent and Central Asia. Though the pass is remote and challenging, it has historical importance in the context of the region’s geography and connectivity. While Khunjerab Pass is another important pass located at the border between Pakistan and China, it does not connect Deosai with Ladakh, but instead links Pakistan's Gilgit-Baltistan region to the Chinese Autonomous Region of Xinjiang. Zoji La Pass and Saltoro Pass are also significant passes in the region but do not provide direct connectivity between Deosai National Park and Ladakh.

questions.question: Where among the following Lakshadweep is located?
questions.explanation: Lakshadweep is a group of islands in the Arabian sea. The entire island group is built of coral deposits. There are approximately 36 islands out of which 10 are inhabited. Minicoy is the second largest island separated from other islands by 9o Channel. Kavaratti is the capital of Lakshadweep. The largest island of Lakshadweep is Andrott. These islands are located at a distance of 220 - 440 km off the Kerala coast.

questions.question: The Narmada and Tapti rivers flank –
questions.explanation: Satpura Range, is part of the Deccan plateau, in central India. The hills stretch through the states of Maharashtra, Madhya Pradesh and Chhattisgarh. The Satpura range, means "Seven Folds", which forms the watershed between the Narmada [North] and Tapti [South] rivers.

questions.question: Rashtriya Arogya Nidhi [RAN] scheme, recently seen in news, is administered by which ministry?
options: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
Ministry of Women and Child Development
questions.explanation: The Delhi High Court recently deemed the new income threshold for the Rashtriya Arogya Nidhi [RAN] scheme as “extremely low.” Under the revised RAN umbrella scheme, the monthly income limit for benefits stands at ₹1,571 for rural areas and ₹1,605 for urban areas. Administered by the Ministry of Health and Family Welfare, RAN provides one-time financial aid to impoverished patients with life-threatening diseases like heart, kidney, liver, and cancer for treatment at specialized government hospitals.

questions.question: Recently, which Indian received the ‘Green Oscar’ Whitley Gold Award 2024?
questions.explanation: Dr. Purnima Devi Barman, an Assamese wildlife biologist, clinched her second Whitley Gold Award, also known as the Green Oscar, for safeguarding the endangered Hargila or Greater Adjutant Stork and its wetland home. She previously won in 2017. Princess Anne, the charity’s patron, presented her the trophy in London. Dr. Barman mobilized locals to protect the stork and its habitat, increasing their population from 450 to over 1800.

questions.question: Who has been appointed as Chairman of DRDO?
questions.explanation: Samir V Kamat: Indian Scientist→ DRDO Chairman Replaced : G Satheesh Reddy About DRDO:- Full Form:- Defence Research and Development Organisation • Founded : 1958 • HQ : New Delhi • Ministry → Ministry Of Defence

questions.question: Which Constitutional Amendment Act dealing with National Judicial Appointments Commission was declared unconstitutional by the Constitution Bench of the Supreme Court?
questions.explanation: The Ninety-ninth Amendment of the Constitution of India, officially known as The Constitution [Ninety ninth Amendment] Act, 2014, formed a National Judicial Appointments Commission. On October 15,2015, a constitutional bench comprising five judges declared the National Judicial Appointments Commission [NJAC] act unconstitutional on the plea that it would affect the independence of the judiciary.

questions.question: Functions to be assigned to Panchayats by 73rd Amendment of the Constitution are mentioned in:
questions.explanation: Panchayat Raj System was given a Constitutional Status by 73rd Amendment by inserting Part IX and 11th Schedule to the Constitution. Seventy-third Amendments, 1992 came into force on 24 April, 1993.

questions.question: Which one of the following is a true fish?
questions.explanation: Starfish or Sea stars are star-shaped echinoderms belonging to the class Asteroidea. Jellyfish [Cnidaria] and Hagfish [Cyclostomata] are also not true fishes. Seahorse is the name given to 45 species of small marine fish in the genus Hippocampus which is a bony fish.

questions.question: The vitamin which is found in citrus fruits and is necessary for maintaining healthy skin is –
questions.explanation: Vitamin C occurs in good quantity in various citrus fruits like lemon, orange, Indian gooseberry [amla] etc. Vitamin C is a water-soluble vitamin which is necessary for normal growth and development and for maintaining healthy skin. Ascorbic acid is the chemical name of Vitamin C. Deficiency of this vitamin causes the disease scurvy in human.

questions.question: Under which plan, for the first time, was the private sector given priority over the public sector?
questions.explanation: Under the Seventh Five- Year Plan, the private sector is given priority over the public sector. The main objectives of the Seventh Five-Year Plan were to establish growth in areas of increasing economic productivity, production of food grains, and generating employment through "Social Justice".

questions.question: The regulatory authority of money supply in India is the:
questions.explanation: The Reserve Bank of India is the regulatory authority responsible for controlling and regulating the money supply in India. a. SBI [State Bank of India] Headquarters: Mumbai, Maharashtra, India Establishment Year: 1955 [as the State Bank of India, though its predecessor, the Bank of Calcutta, was founded in 1806] Relevant Act: The State Bank of India Act, 1955. b. IRDA [Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India] Headquarters: Hyderabad, Telangana, India Establishment Year: 1999 Relevant Act: The Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority Act, 1999. c. RBI [Reserve Bank of India] Headquarters: Mumbai, Maharashtra, India Establishment Year: 1935 Relevant Act: The Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934. d. SEBI [Securities and Exchange Board of India] Headquarters: Mumbai, Maharashtra, India Establishment Year: 1988 [became a statutory body in 1992] Relevant Act: The Securities and Exchange Board of India Act, 1992

questions.question: Which law states that, "At constant temperature, the volume of a given mass of a dry gas is inversely proportional to its pressure"?
questions.explanation: According to the Boyle's law, "At constant temperature T, pressure [P] of a fixed amount of gas varies inversely with its volume [V]. Boyle's Law: At constant temperature, the volume of a given mass of a gas is inversely proportional to its pressure.​ Avogadro's Law: At constant temperature and pressure, the volume of a gas is directly proportional to the number of moles of gas.​​ Charles's Law: At constant pressure, the volume of a gas is directly proportional to its absolute temperature [in Kelvin].​​ Gay-Lussac's Law: At constant volume, the pressure of a gas is directly proportional to its absolute temperature [in Kelvin].

questions.question: What is the familiar name of trichloromethane?
questions.explanation: Trichloromethane is commonly known as Chloroform. It is a colorless, sweet-smelling organic compound often used as a solvent and formerly used as an anesthetic.

questions.question: Which colour of VIBGYOR's light has the minimum wave length?
questions.explanation: In VIBGYOR minimum wavelength is violet colour of light. While the wave length of red colour of light is maximum. The refractive index of a material of a medium depends on the wavelength of the incident light beam. Different colours of light have different wave length.

questions.question: If a vendor sells his goods at profit of 21.2%. If the production cost is increased by 20%, but selling price remains the same. Then profit percentage in this case. > यदि कोई विक्रेता अपना माल 21.2% लाभ पर बेचता है। यदि उत्पादन लागत में 20% की वृद्धि होती है, लेकिन विक्रय मूल्य वही रहता है। तो इस स्थिति में लाभ प्रतिशत क्या होगा?
questions.explanation: Let initial CP=100, then SP=121.2 Increase by 20%, New CP=120 SP=121.2 profit % = ×100=1% > मान लें कि प्रारंभिक CP=100 है, तो SP=121.2 20% की वृद्धि, नया CP=120 SP=121.2 लाभ % = ×100=1%

questions.question: Aman started walking along the diameter of a circular field at a speed of 7 meter per minute and reached at the opposite end in 8 minutes. Then the area of circle in . > अमन ने एक वृत्ताकार मैदान के व्यास के साथ 7 मीटर प्रति मिनट की गति से चलना शुरू किया और 8 मिनट में विपरीत छोर पर पहुँच गया। तो वृत्त का क्षेत्रफल m2 में है।
questions.explanation: AB=7×8=56 meter = diameter Hence, radius =28 meter Area of circular field = ×28×28=2464 > AB=7×8=56 मीटर = व्यास अतः त्रिज्या =28 मीटर वृत्ताकार क्षेत्र का क्षेत्रफल = ×28×28=2464

questions.question: In △ABC, 0 is the incentre and ∠A= 28 ̊, Then what is the measure of ∠BOC?

Rahul can do of a work in 8 days and Ramesh can do of a work in 20 days. They together worked for 5 days and remaining work was completed by Chaman in 5 days. Then find the number of days to complete of work by Rahul and Chaman. > राहुल किसी काम का 40%, 8 दिन में कर सकता है और रमेश किसी काम का , 20 दिन में कर सकता है। उन्होंने मिलकर 5 दिन काम किया और शेष काम चमन ने 5 दिन में पूरा किया। तो राहुल और चमन द्वारा 50% काम पूरा करने में लगने वाले दिनों की संख्या ज्ञात कीजिए।
questions.explanation: Rahul & Ramesh worked for 5 days means they have completed 25 out of 60. Now Remaining 35 is completed by Chaman in 5 days i.e. efficiency of Chaman work will be completed by Rahul & Chaman = × = 3 days > राहुल और रमेश ने 5 दिन काम किया, इसका मतलब है कि उन्होंने 60 में से 25 कार्य पूरे कर लिए हैं। अब शेष 35 कार्य चमन द्वारा 5 दिन में पूरा किया जाएगा अर्थात चमन की कार्यकुशलता = 7 50% कार्य राहुल और चमन द्वारा पूरा किया जाएगा = × = 3 days

questions.explanation: - = 5____ {I} and - = ____ {II} From eqn {I} and {II} Q=40km/hr and P= km/hr Now, Required time = ×23=23hr

questions.question: A tap can fill 12 cm of a bucket in 4 minutes. Find the time taken by the tap to fill the remaining portion of the bucket. {in minutes} [Given that the bucket is of 39 cm]. > एक नल एक बाल्टी का 12 सेमी भाग 4 मिनट में भर सकता है। बाल्टी के शेष भाग को भरने में नल द्वारा लिया गया समय ज्ञात कीजिए। {मिनटों में} [दिया गया है कि बाल्टी 39 सेमी की है]।
questions.explanation: →4 minute → ×27=9 minutes > →4 मिनट → ×27=9 मिनट

questions.question: An article is sold at ₹ 550 for 12% loss. At what price [in ₹] should it be sold to gain 29% of profit in nearest integer? > एक वस्तु को 12% की हानि पर ₹ 550 में बेचा गया। निकटतम पूर्णांक में 29% लाभ प्राप्त करने के लिए इसे किस मूल्य [₹ में] पर बेचा जाना चाहिए?
A 4-digit number x59x is divisible by 36 and a 7-digit number 5y246z6 is divisible by 11. For the maximum value of (y+z) what is the value of (2x2+5y+5z)?
questions.explanation: x59x is divisible by 36, means it is divisible by 4 and 9. For divisibility of 4, last 2 digits should be divisible by 4 and for divisibility of 9, sum of digits should be divisible by 9. So for x=2, both the conditions are satisfied. 5y246z6 is divisible by 11, So the difference between the sum of digits at even and odd place should be 0 or a multiple of 11. So, (y+4+z) - (5+2+6+6) = (y+z) -15 should be 0 or a multiple of 11. y+z -15 = 0,11,22…. So, y+z = 15 (since y and z are also digits so Y+Z≤18) 2x2+5y+5z = 2*22 + 5(15) = 8+75 = 83

questions.question: The table shows the income [in ₹] for a month from different sources of 4 employers [A, B, C & D] The miscellaneous income of C and D is x% of the salary of B, then x lies between
questions.explanation: Miscellaneous income of C&D=9000 Required %=9000/21000×100 =42.85%

questions.question: During a training medical check-up, the heights of 50 trainees were recorded. How many trainees have a height of 165 or more? > प्रशिक्षण चिकित्सा जांच के दौरान 50 प्रशिक्षुओं की लम्बाई दर्ज की गई। कितने प्रशिक्षुओं की ऊंचाई 165 या उससे अधिक है?
questions.explanation: less than165=33 trainee out of 50 Then, trainees with more than 165cm height = 17 > 165 से कम = 50 में से 33 प्रशिक्षु तो, 165 सेमी से अधिक ऊंचाई वाले प्रशिक्षु = 17

In a triangle ABC, xy is drawn parallel to BC, AX=2cm, XB=3cm and XY=4cm, then, the value of BC [in cm] is: > एक त्रिभुज ABC में, xy को BC के समान्तर खींचा गया है, AX=2सेमी, XB=3सेमी और XY=4सेमी है, तो BC का मान [सेमी में] है:

In a cricket match between India and Pakistan, Virat Kohli scored 100 runs which includes 7 fours. What percentage of his total score did he make by running between the wickets? > भारत और पाकिस्तान के बीच एक क्रिकेट मैच में विराट कोहली ने 100 रन बनाए जिसमें 7 चौके शामिल हैं। उन्होंने अपने कुल स्कोर का कितना प्रतिशत विकेटों के बीच दौड़कर बनाया?
questions.explanation: Virat Kohli scored 7 fours i.e. 28 runs by boundaries and 72 runs by running between wickets Required % = ×100 = 72%

questions.explanation: Let original price be 100x, 100x × × = 68x i.e. 32% single discount (मान लें कि मूल कीमत 100x है, > 100x × × = 68x यानी 32% एकल छूट

questions.question: If x = 7+4√3, x>0 then, the value of is:
Four cubes of equal volume are joined end to end to make a solid in the form of cuboid. Find the surface area of the resulting solid if the diagonal of cube is 5√3 cm.
questions.explanation: Diagonal of cube = 5√3 cm side of cube Surface area of resulting cuboid = 4×20×5+2×5×5 = 400+50 = 450cm2

questions.question: In a boat race, a person can row 40 km upstream and 55 km downstream in 13 hours. Also, he can sow 30 km upstream and 44 km downstream in 10 hours. Find the speed of stream. > एक नाव दौड़ में, एक व्यक्ति 13 घंटे में 40 किमी धारा के प्रतिकूल और 55 किमी धारा के अनुकूल नाव चला सकता है। साथ ही, वह 10 घंटे में 30 किमी धारा के प्रतिकूल और 44 किमी धारा के अनुकूल नाव चला सकता है। धारा की गति ज्ञात कीजिए।
questions.explanation: Let speed upstream be x and downstream speed be y then, + =13 and + =10 by solving equations, we get y =11km/hr & x=5km/hr Speed of stream = [y-x] = [11 5] = 3km/hr = = 50m/min > धारा के प्रतिकूल गति x और धारा के अनुकूल गति y मान लें, तो + =13 और + =10 समीकरणों को हल करके, हम पाते हैं y =11km/hr और x = 5km/hr धारा की गति = [y-x] = [11 5] = 3km/hr = = 50m/min

questions.question: The effective annual rate of interest corresponding to a nominal rate of per annum payable half-yeally is: > अर्धवार्षिक रूप से देय 4% प्रति वर्ष की नाममात्र दर के अनुरूप प्रभावी वार्षिक ब्याज दर है:
questions.explanation: Let the sum be 100x and other compounded half yearly rate become 2% and time become 2 So, Amount after one year = 100x =100x × × =104.04x Hence effective rate = ×100= 4.04% > मान लें कि राशि 100x है और अन्य चक्रवृद्धि अर्धवार्षिक दर 2% हो जाती है और समय 2 हो जाता है तो, एक वर्ष के बाद राशि = 100x =100x × × =104.04x इसलिए प्रभावी दर = ×100= 4.04%

questions.question: Identify the word that is incorrectly spelt.
questions.explanation: The correct spelling is – Coalescence Let’s see the meaning of all words, Antiseptic - एंटीसेप्टिक - A substance used to prevent infection. Coalescence - सम्मिलन - The process of merging or coming together. Bizarre - अजीब - Very strange or unusual. Diarrhoea - दस्त - Frequent and watery bowel movements.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the underlined word in the following sentence - She gave a demure smile when asked about her achievements
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Sober Let’s see the meaning of all words, Demure - संकोची - Reserved, shy, or modest in manner. Sober - गंभीर - Serious, not affected by alcohol, or calm in demeanor. Bold - साहसी - Showing courage, willingness to take risks, or being daring. Wily - चालाक - Skilled at using clever tricks to achieve a goal. Bleak - नीरस - Lacking in hope or cheer; dreary.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate antonym of the given word. FATUOUS
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Sensible Let’s see the meaning of all words, Fatuous - मूर्ख - Silly or foolish, lacking intelligence or awareness. Puerile - बालसुलभ - Childish, immature, or juvenile in behavior. Lethargy - निद्रालुता - A state of sluggishness, inactivity, or lack of energy. Sensible - समझदार - Showing good judgment or wisdom; practical and reasonable. Crisp - ताज़ा - Firm, fresh, and easily broken; often used to describe food or weather.

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to make the sentence sensibly correct. The captain decided to Embrace the excess cargo to lighten the ship during the storm.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Jettison Let’s see the meaning of all words, Embrace - गले लगाना - To accept or adopt something willingly, or to hug someone. Jettison - त्यागना - To throw or discard something, often to lighten a load or get rid of something unwanted. Inscrutable - रहस्यमय - Impossible to understand or interpret; mysterious. Receive - प्राप्त करना - To get or accept something that is given or sent. Endorse - समर्थन करना - To publicly approve or support something or someone.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning for the given idiom. FEEL THE PINCH
questions.explanation: The correct meaning is – Financial difficulties Feel the pinch - तंगी महसूस करना - To experience financial difficulty or hardship.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate SYNONYM of the given word. IMPETUOUS
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Expedite Let’s see the meaning of all words, Impetuous - अविचारपूर्ण - Acting quickly without thinking or care. Expedite - त्वरित करना - To make a process or action happen faster. Cautious - सावधान - Being careful to avoid risks or mistakes. Diminutive - सूक्ष्म - Extremely small or tiny. Haggard - थका-हारा - Looking tired, worn, or unhealthy, usually due to worry or illness.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate antonym of the underlined word in the following sentence- The coach began to vituperate the players after their poor performance, criticizing them harshly for their mistakes.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Accolade Vituperate - निंदा करना - To criticize or speak about someone in an extremely harsh or abusive manner. Harness - कसकर बांधना - To control or make use of something effectively, especially power or energy. Accolade - पुरस्कार - An award or privilege granted in recognition of merit. Reinstate - फिर से बहाल करना - To restore someone or something to a previous position or state. Exultation - आनंद - A feeling of great joy, happiness, or celebration.

questions.question: Select the word which means the same as the group of words. Interval between two reigns
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Interregnum Let’s see the meaning of all words, Interregnum - कालांतराल - A period of time between the end of one reign or government and the beginning of another, or a gap in continuity. Innuendo - आरोप - An indirect or subtle remark, usually implying something negative or suggestive. Interlude - विश्रामकाल - A period of time between two events or parts of a performance; a pause or break. Nemesis - प्रतिशोध - A long-standing rival or an agent of retribution, often representing justice or vengeance.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. At a stretch
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Continuously At a stretch - एक साथ - Without stopping; continuously or for an extended period of time, often beyond what is comfortable or expected.

questions.question: Select the word which means the same as the group of words given. Widespread outbreak of a disease affects large population at the same time.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is – Epidemic Let’s see the meaning of all words, Pandemic - महामारी - A disease that spreads across a large geographical area, affecting many people globally. Emeritus - मानद - A title given to a retired person, especially a professor, who retains their title as an honor. Endemic - स्थानीय - A disease or condition regularly found and native to a particular area or population. Epidemic - महामारी - A sudden outbreak of a disease affecting a large number of people in a specific region or community.

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error.
questions.explanation: The error lies in segment: was late is because Explanation: • The phrase "is because" is incorrect when used after the noun "reason". • Using "because" after "the reason" is redundant, as both imply cause. • The grammatically correct structure after "the reason" is to use "that", not "because". • The correct structure is: "The reason [subject + verb] is that..." Corrected Sentence: The reason she was late is that the bus broke down.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. Instead of tea or coffee, drink water to _________ your thirst.
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: Quench Explanation: The word "quench" is the correct choice as it means to satisfy thirst. It fits perfectly in this context, where the sentence is about drinking water to satisfy or stop feeling thirsty. Here are the meanings and examples of all options: • Calm – शांत करना – Means to make something peaceful or relaxed. Example: She tried to calm her nerves before the exam. • Ease – कम करना – Means to reduce something unpleasant like pain or tension. Example: He took medicine to ease the pain. • Quest – खोज, तलाश – Means a search or pursuit, especially something difficult or important. Example: They went on a quest for hidden treasure. • Quench – प्यास बुझाना – This is the correct word in this context as it means to satisfy thirst. Example: A glass of cold water quenched his thirst after the run. Final Correct Sentence: Instead of tea or coffee, drink water to quench your thirst.

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence. In case no improvement is needed, select 'No improvement required'. The teacher gave us several informations during the lecture.
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: Several pieces of information Explanation: • The word "information" is an uncountable noun, and it does not have a plural form like "informations". • To express a plural quantity of an uncountable noun, we use unit phrases such as "a piece of," "some," or "several pieces of." • Option [A] "An information" is incorrect because "information" cannot take an article like "an." • Option [C] "Many informations" is incorrect as "informations" is not a valid word. • Therefore, "several pieces of information" is the correct and grammatically accurate expression. Corrected Sentence: The teacher gave us several pieces of information during the lecture.

questions.question: Select the Grammatically correct sentence -
options: He prefers walking in the park to jogging in the morning.
He prefers to walk in the park than jog in the morning.
He prefers walking in the park than jogging in the morning.
He prefers to walking in the park than jogging in the morning.
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: He prefers walking in the park to jogging in the morning. Explanation: • This sentence is grammatically correct and follows the rule for using prefer to compare two activities. • The correct structure is: prefer + gerund + to + gerund [e.g., walking to jogging]. • Options [B], [C], and [D] are incorrect because they use "than" instead of "to" or have wrong verb forms [infinitive vs. gerund]. • "He prefers walking in the park to jogging in the morning." properly compares two activities using the gerund form and the preposition "to." Concept Applied: • Structure: prefer + gerund + to + gerund • Usage: To express preference between two activities

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence. In case no improvement is needed, select 'No improvement required'. They set up early in the morning to reach the mountain summit by noon. > Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence. In case no improvement is needed, select 'No improvement required'. They set up early in the morning to reach the mountain summit by noon.
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: Set out Explanation: The phrasal verb "set out" means to start a journey or begin traveling towards a destination, which fits this sentence describing someone starting early to reach the mountain summit. Meanings and examples of the options: • Set out – यात्रा शुरू करना – To start a journey Example: They set out early in the morning to reach the mountain summit by noon. • Set off – निकलना, शुरू करना – To begin a journey or cause something to happen Example: We set off on our trip at dawn. • Set up – स्थापित करना, व्यवस्था करना – To arrange or organize something Example: They set up the tent before nightfall. • Set after – पीछा करना – To chase someone or something Example: The dog set after the cat. Final Correct Sentence: They set out early in the morning to reach the mountain summit by noon. >Correct Answer: Set out Explanation: The phrasal verb "set out" means to start a journey or begin traveling towards a destination, which fits this sentence describing someone starting early to reach the mountain summit. Meanings and examples of the options: • Set out – यात्रा शुरू करना – To start a journey Example: They set out early in the morning to reach the mountain summit by noon. • Set off – निकलना, शुरू करना – To begin a journey or cause something to happen Example: We set off on our trip at dawn. • Set up – स्थापित करना, व्यवस्था करना – To arrange or organize something Example: They set up the tent before nightfall. • Set after – पीछा करना – To chase someone or something Example: The dog set after the cat. Final Correct Sentence: They set out early in the morning to reach the mountain summit by noon.

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to make the sentence sensibly correct. Many athletes compete _____ the gold medal in the Olympic Games.
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: For Explanation: • The verb phrase "compete for" is used when people or teams try to win or gain something, such as a prize, award, or position. • "Compete for" expresses the goal or object that participants aim to achieve by competing. Examples: • Many candidates compete for the same job position. • The teams compete for the championship trophy. • In contrast, "compete with" means to be rivals against someone, but it doesn’t specify what is being contested. • "Compete to" or "compete on" are not correct collocations in this context. Corrected Sentence: Many athletes compete for the gold medal in the Olympic Games.

questions.question: Identify the segment in the sentence that contains grammatical error.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: strong reasons Explanation: • The structure "enough + noun" is correct, so the word "enough" should come before the noun, not before the adjective. • In this sentence, "enough" is incorrectly placed before the adjective "strong." • The correct order is: enough reasons [noun] + with/without adjectives like "strong." Examples: • She had enough reasons to quit. ✔ • She had strong enough reasons to quit. ✔ • She had enough strong reasons to quit. ❌ Corrected Sentence: She had strong enough reasons to quit the job immediately.

questions.question: Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful paragraph: A. It means that you’re good at making time for what matters to you B. Especially when you don’t feel like it C. Instead of playing the role of the victim and letting life happen to you D. Becoming a pro doesn’t mean you’re a workaholic
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: DABC Explanation: Let’s break it down step by step: • Sentence D introduces the main idea that becoming a pro doesn’t mean being a workaholic, setting a clear and thoughtful tone for the paragraph. • Sentence A follows naturally by clarifying what being a pro does mean—it means making time for what matters, adding definition to the initial idea. • Sentence B adds depth, highlighting the importance of consistency even when motivation is low. • Sentence C concludes the paragraph powerfully by contrasting the proactive mind-set of a professional with the passive role of a victim, reinforcing the message. Final Paragraph: Becoming a pro doesn’t mean you’re a workaholic. It means that you’re good at making time for what matters to you, especially when you don’t feel like it. Instead of playing the role of the victim and letting life happen to you.

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to change the voice of the given sentence. The team had solved the issue before the meeting began. > Choose the most appropriate option to change the voice of given sentence. The team had solved the issue before the meeting began.
options: The issue was solved by the team before the meeting began.
The issue is being solved by the team before the meeting began.
The issue has been solved by the team before the meeting began.
The issue had been solved by the team before the meeting began.
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: The issue had been solved by the team before the meeting began. Explanation: • The sentence is in past perfect tense: "had solved" • The correct passive structure for past perfect is: had + been + past participle • "Solve" becomes "been solved" • The agent "by the team" is properly included • Time clause "before the meeting began" remains unchanged Corrected Sentence: The issue had been solved by the team before the meeting began. >Correct Answer: The issue had been solved by the team before the meeting began. questions.question: Change the narration of the given sentence. The doctor advised the patient, "Take your medicines regularly."
options: The doctor advised the patient to take his medicines regularly.
The doctor told the patient that he should takes his medicines regularly.
The doctor said the patient to take his medicines regularly.
The doctor advised the patient that he takes his medicines regularly.
questions.explanation: Explanation: • The reporting verb "advised" is used when giving suggestions or recommendations. • In indirect speech, an imperative sentence is changed into an infinitive phrase with "to" + verb. • "Take" becomes "to take" in the reported speech. • The pronoun "your" changes to "his" to match the subject "the patient." • Options [B], [C], and [D] have errors in verb form or sentence structure. Final Sentence: The doctor advised the patient to take his medicines regularly.

questions.question: Motivation is the driving force that ____1____ us to achieve our goals and pursue our passions. It's the spark that ignites the fire ____2____ us, pushing us to take action, overcome obstacles, and ____3____ for excellence. Without motivation, even the most talented and skilled individuals may struggle to reach their full potential. There are two types of motivation: intrinsic and extrinsic. Intrinsic motivation comes from within, ____4____ by personal interests, values, and passions. Extrinsic motivation, on the other hand, is ____5____ by external factors such as rewards, recognition, and social pressure. While both types of motivation can be effective, research suggests that intrinsic motivation is a more sustainable and powerful driver of success. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 1 -
questions.explanation: Explanation - The word "propels" means "to drive or push something forward" which fits perfectly in this context, implying that motivation is the force that drives us to take action and achieve our goals. The other options don't fit as well: - Excels: Means "to be exceptionally good at something" which doesn't fit the context. - Progress: Is a noun, and doesn't fit grammatically in this sentence. - Expels: Means "to force something or someone out" which is the opposite of what the sentence is trying to convey.

questions.question: Motivation is the driving force that ____1____ us to achieve our goals and pursue our passions. It's the spark that ignites the fire ____2____ us, pushing us to take action, overcome obstacles, and ____3____ for excellence. Without motivation, even the most talented and skilled individuals may struggle to reach their full potential. There are two types of motivation: intrinsic and extrinsic. Intrinsic motivation comes from within, ____4____ by personal interests, values, and passions. Extrinsic motivation, on the other hand, is ____5____ by external factors such as rewards, recognition, and social pressure. While both types of motivation can be effective, research suggests that intrinsic motivation is a more sustainable and powerful driver of success. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 2 -
questions.explanation: Explanation - The phrase "within us" means "inside us" or "deep down" which fits perfectly in this context, implying that the fire of motivation burns from within. The other options don't fit as well: - In: Could work in some contexts, but "within" is a better fit here. - Into: Implies movement or direction, which doesn't fit the context. - With into: Doesn't make grammatical sense in this sentence.

questions.question: Motivation is the driving force that ____1____ us to achieve our goals and pursue our passions. It's the spark that ignites the fire ____2____ us, pushing us to take action, overcome obstacles, and ____3____ for excellence. Without motivation, even the most talented and skilled individuals may struggle to reach their full potential. There are two types of motivation: intrinsic and extrinsic. Intrinsic motivation comes from within, ____4____ by personal interests, values, and passions. Extrinsic motivation, on the other hand, is ____5____ by external factors such as rewards, recognition, and social pressure. While both types of motivation can be effective, research suggests that intrinsic motivation is a more sustainable and powerful driver of success. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 3 -
questions.explanation: Explanation – The word "strive" means "to make an effort or struggle towards a goal" which fits perfectly in this context, implying that motivation drives us to work towards achieving excellence. The other options don't fit as well: - Achieve: Implies success or accomplishment, which doesn't fit the context of working towards a goal. - Conceive: Means "to think of or imagine something" which doesn't fit the context. - Deceive: Means "to mislead or cheat" which is the opposite of what the sentence is trying to convey.

questions.question: Motivation is the driving force that ____1____ us to achieve our goals and pursue our passions. It's the spark that ignites the fire ____2____ us, pushing us to take action, overcome obstacles, and ____3____ for excellence. Without motivation, even the most talented and skilled individuals may struggle to reach their full potential. There are two types of motivation: intrinsic and extrinsic. Intrinsic motivation comes from within, ____4____ by personal interests, values, and passions. Extrinsic motivation, on the other hand, is ____5____ by external factors such as rewards, recognition, and social pressure. While both types of motivation can be effective, research suggests that intrinsic motivation is a more sustainable and powerful driver of success. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 4 -
questions.explanation: Explanation – The word "fueled" means "to provide energy or motivation" which fits perfectly in this context, implying that personal interests, values, and passions drive and energize intrinsic motivation. The other options don't fit as well: - Exaggerated: Means "overstated or magnified" which doesn't fit the context. - Forced: Implies coercion or artificial pressure, which doesn't fit the context of intrinsic motivation. - Expelled: Means "forced out" which doesn't fit the context.

questions.question: Motivation is the driving force that ____1____ us to achieve our goals and pursue our passions. It's the spark that ignites the fire ____2____ us, pushing us to take action, overcome obstacles, and ____3____ for excellence. Without motivation, even the most talented and skilled individuals may struggle to reach their full potential. There are two types of motivation: intrinsic and extrinsic. Intrinsic motivation comes from within, ____4____ by personal interests, values, and passions. Extrinsic motivation, on the other hand, is ____5____ by external factors such as rewards, recognition, and social pressure. While both types of motivation can be effective, research suggests that intrinsic motivation is a more sustainable and powerful driver of success. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 5 -
questions.explanation: Explanation – The word "driven" means "to be motivated or propelled" which fits perfectly in this context, implying that external factors motivate extrinsic motivation. The other options don't fit as well: - Drived: Isn't a word (the correct verb form would be "driven"). - Drove: Is a verb in the past tense, which doesn't fit the context. - Driving: Could work in some contexts, but "driven" is a better fit here, as it implies a passive relationship between extrinsic motivation and external factors
